
# Project Lighthouse — Kickoff Meeting
NOTE: This is an AI-generated test document, it's not real, and any resembelenace to real people or places is coincidence. 

**Date:** 2026-05-04
**Attendees:** Sarah Chen (Product Manager), Raj Patel (Lead Engineer), Elena Torres (Design Lead),
David Kim (Stakeholder, VP of Operations), Marcus Webb (Backend Engineer)

## Agenda
- Introduce Project Lighthouse: a rebuild of the internal inventory dashboard for Nimbus Analytics.
- Align on scope for the V1 milestone.
- Assign workstreams.

## Discussion
Sarah opened by framing the goal: the current inventory dashboard (built in 2019) is on a
deprecated internal framework and can't support the new warehouse locations David's team is
onboarding next quarter. David confirmed the Denver and Reno warehouses go live August 2026,
so V1 needs to at least handle multi-warehouse views by then.

Raj raised concerns about the existing data pipeline — it's a nightly batch job that won't
support the near-real-time stock counts David's team wants. Marcus volunteered to spike on
a streaming ingestion approach using the existing Kafka cluster.

Elena walked through some early wireframes for the multi-warehouse selector and flagged that
she'll need finalized warehouse naming conventions from David's team before she can finish
the navigation design.

## Action Items
- Raj: Draft technical design doc for the data pipeline rewrite, due 2026-05-11.
- Marcus: Spike streaming ingestion with Kafka, report back at next sync.
- Elena: Follow up with David's team for warehouse naming conventions.
- Sarah: Circulate V1 scope doc to stakeholders by end of week.

## Next Meeting
2026-05-11, same time.
